title: Week of 22 June
intro: Mid-week update: built from what we already bought, with the first few days logged as eaten. Leaning into what the kids actually like — Grandma's creamy carrots & potatoes. (Also had broccoli with miso dip and a radish–cucumber salad earlier in the week.)

days.dish: Oyster mushrooms in a Japanese-style sauce with rice
days.core: Parents: oyster mushrooms simmered in a soy–ginger sauce, served over rice.
days.kids: Potatoes with quark, and champignons with cheese — both largely rejected this time.

days.dish: Millet (Hirse) with white bean paste, roasted pumpkin & zucchini
days.core: Parents: millet with a white-bean paste alongside roasted pumpkin and zucchini.

Sweat finely chopped onion in olive oil until glassy. Add diced carrots and potatoes and let them take on a little colour, then add a little vegetable broth dissolved in water. In a second pot, melt butter, stir in ½–1 Tbsp flour and cook briefly, then whisk in soy milk (or milk) to a sauce consistency; season with a little Maggi, paprika and a pinch of nutmeg. Fold the sauce through the vegetables. Quick version: stir a little flour into cold water, add to the veg with milk/soy and spices, and simmer to a sauce. Serve as is or with rice.
days.kids: This is the one they like — keep it simple, just carrots & potatoes.
days.dairy: Stir a little cream or quark into yours.
days.toddler: Ideal for the little one — soft, mild, low salt; go easy on the Maggi.

days.dish: Aubergine & chickpea tomato stew with spaghetti
days.core: Sauté onion, garlic and ginger, add diced aubergine, baby tomatoes and the jar of chickpeas, and simmer into a soft stew. Serve over spaghetti or rice.
days.toddler: Mash the stew, mild spice, soft aubergine cut small.

days.core: Sauté shimeji and champignons with garlic and ginger, season with soy sauce and a touch of sweetness, and serve over rice.
days.toddler: Chop the mushrooms small (they're chewy) and keep it mild.

days.core: Indian-style onion–tomato masala with cumin, coriander and turmeric; add the second aubergine and potatoes and cook until soft. Serve with rice.
Same creamy flour + soy/milk sauce as Wednesday, this time with the last of the mushrooms and carrots, tossed through pasta.
days.kids: They like creamy — finish with a little cheese.

vesper.note: Older one's snack box, 5 days a week. A bit bigger on Tuesday and Thursday. Savoury only — no sweet muffins etc.

days.idea: Carrot & cucumber sticks, bread with butter & honey-margarine, a few almonds.
Pretzel with Wurst & margarine, mini tomato halves, boiled egg slices, a small handful of cashews.
Bread with Aufstrich, carrot sticks, mini tomato halves, a few almonds.
Bread roll with Wurst & butter, boiled egg slices, carrot & cucumber sticks, baby tomatoes, cashews.
Pretzel–Wurst–margarine, carrot sticks, mini tomato halves, a few cashews.

items: Maggi, paprika, nutmeg, cumin, coriander, turmeric

preptip: The creamy flour-and-milk/soy sauce (Grandma's method) is the backbone twice this week — Wednesday's carrots & potatoes and Sunday's mushroom pasta. Make a little extra on Wednesday and keep it for Sunday. Boil a few eggs at the start of the week for both dinners and the Vesper box.
